title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who took part in an extreme sports challenge. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Liam, The Mountain Ultramarathon
'I thought my marathon training would be enough, but I was wrong,' Liam admits. [47]The vertical gain was so intense that it felt more like mountaineering than running, which I hadn't prepared for.[/47] [53]I learned that the body is capable of much more than the mind believes; you just have to silence the inner critic.[/53] [51]The most difficult moment was navigating in the dark during a storm, which was terrifying.[/51] [50]I found that the focus I used in my previous career as a high-altitude pilot was essential for staying calm under pressure.[/50] [55]It took me several days to stop feeling a strange sense of guilt for resting after the race ended.[/55] It was a brutal but enlightening experience.
texts.B:
### Sarah, The Ocean Swim
Sarah swam across a major strait. 'The water was freezing,' she recalls. [48]I was surprised to find that my breathing became much more efficient as a result of the cold-water exposure.[/48] [52]I hadn't anticipated the mental toll of seeing nothing but blue for six hours; it was incredibly disorienting.[/52] [54]What kept me going was the support of the boat crew, who cheered every time I looked up.[/54] [49]The experience taught me to appreciate the power of nature and my own small place within it.[/49] [56]If I hadn't been part of a local swimming club, I never would have had the courage to sign up.[/56] [50]I’ve noticed that the stamina I built has made me much more energetic in my daily life as a teacher.
texts.C:
### Marcus, The Desert Trek
Marcus walked 200 kilometers across a desert. 'The heat was a physical enemy,' he notes. [51]I struggled with the isolation, as I didn't see another human being for three days at one point.[/51] [47]I hadn't expected the sand to be so difficult to walk on; it was like moving through thick soup.[/47] [53]I realized that my happiness isn't tied to comfort, but to a sense of purpose and progress.[/53] [48]I noticed that my chronic knee pain completely disappeared, likely due to the weight loss and steady movement.[/48] [55]It took a while to adjust back to the noise of the city, which felt aggressive after the silence.[/55] [49]The trip changed my perspective on what constitutes a 'need' versus a 'want'.
texts.D:
### Elena, The Long-Distance Cycle
Elena cycled across a continent. 'It was a test of equipment and spirit,' she says. [52]I hadn't planned for the mechanical failures, which forced me to learn basic engineering on the side of the road.[/52] [50]I found that the organizational skills from my job as a logistics manager were perfect for planning my daily stops.[/50] [54]I was moved by the kindness of strangers who offered me water and food in the most remote areas.[/54] [48]I was amazed by how much my sleep quality improved; I haven't slept so deeply in years.[/48] [56]I wouldn't have known about this route if I hadn't stumbled upon a blog post by a former cyclist.[/56] [49]It was an exercise in living in the present moment, mile by mile.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by a specific physical difficulty of the terrain?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) was surprised by the vertical gain, and Marcus (C) by the difficulty of walking on sand.

questions.question: notes an unexpected physical benefit resulting from the challenge?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions efficient breathing, Marcus (C) mentions knee pain vanishing, and Elena (D) mentions better sleep.

questions.question: expresses a change in their general philosophy or outlook on life?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) appreciates her place in nature, Marcus (C) redefined 'needs' vs 'wants', and Elena (D) lives in the present.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background or skills proved useful?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) used his pilot skills, and Elena (D) used her logistics management skills.

questions.question: describes a challenge that was particularly frightening or lonely?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) mentions the dark storm, and Marcus (C) mentions the three days of isolation.

questions.question: admits to being unprepared for a specific mental or technical problem?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions the disorientation of seeing only blue, and Elena (D) mentions mechanical failures.

questions.question: reflects on a deeper psychological lesson learned about themselves?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) learned about the body's capability, and Marcus (C) realized happiness isn't tied to comfort.

questions.question: mentions the importance of external support or kindness from others?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions her boat crew, and Elena (D) mentions the kindness of strangers.

questions.question: describes a period of adjustment or a strange feeling after the event ended?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) felt guilt for resting, and Marcus (C) found city noise aggressive.

questions.question: acknowledges the source of their inspiration or the help that made it possible?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) credits her swimming club, and Elena (D) credits a blog post she found.
